incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 2 instead of scale of 1

steps.text: You chose scale of 2. But the question asks you to toggle to 1. Look at the three options and click on scale of 1.

incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 5 instead of scale of 1

steps.text: The graph is already at scale of 5. The question asks you to change it to 1. Click on scale of 1.

incorrects.description: Student did not select scale of 1 after multiple attempts

params.description: Graph animates to scale of 1. Symbols multiply: Olivia from 8 to 40, Liam from 7 to 35, Noah from 5 to 25, Emma from 4 to 20. Key updates to Each star = 1.

steps.text: When you toggle to scale of 1, each symbol represents just 1 book. So Olivia's 40 books need 40 symbols. That's why the graph gets so long.

incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 1 instead of scale of 2

steps.text: That's scale of 1, where you just were. The question asks you to toggle to 2. Click on scale of 2.

incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 5 instead of scale of 2

steps.text: That's scale of 5. We'll go there soon. First, try scale of 2. Click on scale of 2.

incorrects.description: Student did not select scale of 2 after multiple attempts

params.description: Graph animates to scale of 2. Symbols reduce: Olivia from 40 to 20, Liam from 35 to 17.5 with half-symbol, Noah from 25 to 12.5 with half-symbol, Emma from 20 to 10. Key updates to Each star = 2.

steps.text: At scale of 2, each symbol represents 2 books. So Olivia's 40 books need 20 symbols. That's half as many as scale of 1, but still more than scale of 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 1 instead of scale of 5

steps.text: You went back to scale of 1, the longest one. The question asks you to toggle to 5. Click on scale of 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected scale of 2 instead of scale of 5

steps.text: That's scale of 2, where you just were. The question asks you to go to 5. Click on scale of 5.

incorrects.description: Student did not select scale of 5 after multiple attempts

params.description: Graph animates to scale of 5. Symbols reduce: Olivia from 20 to 8, Liam from 17.5 to 7, Noah from 12.5 to 5, Emma from 10 to 4. Key updates to Each star = 5.

steps.text: At scale of 5, each symbol represents 5 books. Olivia's 40 books need just 8 symbols. That's the original graph you built, the shortest version.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 1, which uses the most symbols

steps.text: Look at Olivia's row at each scale. At scale of 1, she had 40 symbols. At scale of 5, she had only 8. Which is fewer?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2, which uses more symbols than scale of 5

steps.text: At scale of 2, Olivia had 20 symbols. At scale of 5, she had 8 symbols. Which number is smaller, 20 or 8?

incorrects.description: Student did not select Scale of 5 after multiple attempts

params.description: Visual shows side-by-side comparison: Scale 1 with 40 symbols for Olivia, Scale 2 with 20 symbols, Scale 5 with 8 symbols.

steps.text: Scale of 5 uses the fewest symbols. When each symbol represents MORE books, you need FEWER symbols. That's why bigger scales are useful when numbers get large.
